steps.title: Set up the tangency condition using substitution
steps.content:
The system has exactly one distinct real solution when the horizontal line $y = -1.5$ touches the parabola at exactly one point (tangency). Substitute the first equation into the second:
$$-1.5 = x^2 + 8x + a$$
Rearrange to standard form:
$$x^2 + 8x + (a + 1.5) = 0$$
For this quadratic to have exactly one real solution, the discriminant must equal zero.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition and solve for a
steps.content:
For the quadratic $x^2 + 8x + (a + 1.5) = 0$ to have exactly one real solution, set the discriminant to zero:
$$8^2 - 4(1)(a + 1.5) = 0$$
$$64 - 4a - 6 = 0$$
$$58 = 4a$$
$$a = 14.5$$
